mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals tightening labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 5%, or the signal's IC drops below 0.05 in 1-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in real GDP (GDPC1) above 3% signals strong economic momentum that drives equity risk premiums higher, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX fails to generate positive 60d returns in 50%+ of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 3%, or the signal's IC falls below 0.04 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and declining real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate a supply-side shock that increases corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO rises and RSAFS falls, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in CPI inflation above 2% signals central bank hawkishness that raises real interest rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2%, or the signal's IC drops below -0.04 in 1-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 500k units signals weak consumer demand that depresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where HOUST falls below 500k, or the signal's IC drops below -0.05 in 1-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above 200k units signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 200k, or the signal's IC drops below 0.04 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 95% of its 12-month moving average indicates weak supply chain conditions that depress corporate profits, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO falls below 95% of its 12-month MA, or the signal's IC drops below -0.03 in 2-year rolling windows.
